code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
    Unknown predicates fall back to a generic pattern.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping for more natural wording
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": "{s} serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "The elevation of {s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "The elevation of {s} is {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "The elevation of {s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": "{s} is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "{s} is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": "The runway length at {s} is {o} metres",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "The runway length at {s} is {o} feet",
        "runwayName": "{s} has runway {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "{s} has a first runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "{s} has a first runway length of {o} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "{s} has first runway number {o}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a first runway surface of {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "{s} has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "{s} has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "The fourth runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "{s} has fifth runway number {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "{s} has ICAO identifier {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": "{s} has location identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "{s} has IATA identifier {o}",
        "nativeName": "{s} is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": "{s} is governed by the {o} party",
        "capital": "{o} is the capital of {s}",
        "language": "The main language spoken in {s} is {o}",
        "leader": "{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": "{s} has largest city {o}",
        "mayor": "{s} has mayor {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "{s} has official language {o}",
        "city": "{s} is a city {o}",
        "jurisdiction": "{s} falls under jurisdiction of {o}",
        "demonym": "{s} residents are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "{s} operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": "{s} operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": "{s} uses the {o}",
        "headquarter": "{s} is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": "{s} belongs to class {o}",
        "division": "{s} belongs to division {o}",
        "order": "{s} belongs to the order of {o}",
        "regionServed": "{s} serves the region {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "{s} has leader title {o}",
        "hubAirport": "{s} uses hub airport {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": "{s} flies fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": "{s} flies attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": "{s} participated in {o}",
        "countySeat": "{s} has county seat {o}",
        "chief": "{s} is led by chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": "{s} was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": "{s} has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": "{s} has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "{s} is in ceremonial county {o}",
    }

    # Group phrase fragments by subject, handling runway attributes specially
    subject_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    runway_attrs: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = {}  # subject -> ordinal -> attribute dict

    for t in triples:
        obj = t.object.strip().strip('\"').strip('\'')
        # Detect runway‑related predicates
        if any(key in t.predicate for key in ["RunwaySurfaceType", "RunwayLength", "RunwayLengthFeet", "RunwayNumber"]):
            import re
            m = re.match(r"(\\d+(st|nd|rd|th))", t.predicate)
            ordinal = m.group(1) if m else "1st"
            runway_attrs.setdefault(t.subject, {}).setdefault(ordinal, {})
            if "SurfaceType" in t.predicate:
                runway_attrs[t.subject][ordinal]["surface"] = obj
            elif "LengthFeet" in t.predicate:
                runway_attrs[t.subject][ordinal]["length_feet"] = obj
            elif "Length" in t.predicate and "Feet" not in t.predicate:
                runway_attrs[t.subject][ordinal]["length_metres"] = obj
            elif "Number" in t.predicate:
                runway_attrs[t.subject][ordinal]["number"] = obj
            continue  # runway info will be rendered later
        # Generic handling
        tmpl = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            phrase = tmpl.format(s=t.subject, o=obj)
            if phrase.startswith(t.subject):
                phrase = phrase[len(t.subject):].lstrip()
        else:
            phrase = f"{t.predicate} {obj}"
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(phrase)

    # Add compact runway descriptions
    for subj, ord_dict in runway_attrs.items():
        runway_sentences = []
        for ordinal, attrs in ord_dict.items():
            parts = []
            if "number" in attrs:
                parts.append(f"runway number {attrs['number']}")
            if "surface" in attrs:
                parts.append(f"surface {attrs['surface']}")
            if "length_metres" in attrs:
                parts.append(f"{attrs['length_metres']} metres long")
            if "length_feet" in attrs:
                parts.append(f"{attrs['length_feet']} feet long")
            description = f"The {ordinal} runway at {subj} is " + " and ".join(parts)
            runway_sentences.append(description)
        # Append the combined runway description to the subject's fragments
        subject_map.setdefault(subj, []).append("; ".join(runway_sentences))

    # Build a sentence for each subject, joining its fragments with commas and 'and'
    sentences = []
    for subj, parts in subject_map.items():
        if len(parts) == 1:
            combined = parts[0]
        else:
            combined = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + " and " + parts[-1]
        sentences.append(f"{subj} {combined}")

    # Join different subjects with semicolons
    sentence = "; ".join(sentences) + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
